title: activity — error catalogue
description: Error codes this module emits. `user_facing` controls whether the message renders to end users (translated via i18n). `retryable` signals client-side retry. `audit_severity` routes to the correct log channel — `alert` pages on-call, `critical` fires the security-incident notification pipeline.

errors.description: Someone attempted to hard-delete an activity row from outside the retention pipeline. Enforced at the observer layer with a static caller-marker check. Warns because it's a bug (some code path is trying to bypass retention).
POST / PATCH / DELETE against /api/v1/activities/* returns 405 with this code — writes happen exclusively via the vendor LogsActivity trait on domain models.

Referenced log_name is not in data/log-names-catalog.json. Almost always indicates a typo OR a missing catalog registration when a downstream module adds a new feed. Refused by valid_log_name rule + at model boot when LogsActivity's useLogName() names an unregistered value.
retention_days > 90 without the `activity_retention_extended` entitlement (Enterprise). Payload includes the entitlement key so the frontend can offer an upgrade CTA.
retention_days outside 30..730. 30 is the floor (never shorter than 1 month of forensic memory); 730 is the regulator-hold ceiling.

retention_days > 365 without expires_at set. Regulator holds SHOULD document a review-by date so the override doesn't drift into permanent by default. Warn severity — this is a defensive check catching missing due-diligence.
A retention policy for this (tenant, log_name) pair already exists. Delete the existing one first if the intent is to reset.
Attempt to opt into digest email without the `activity_digest_email` entitlement (Medium+).

Attempt to read an activity from a different tenant (bypassing BelongsToTenant scope via a raw ID query). Should be impossible at runtime — the observer refuses; this is defence in depth. Fires a security-incident notification.
WebSocket subscription failed authorization on tenant.{id}.activity or user.{id}.activity. Refused by the reverb channel auth callback.
ActivityBatchStarted fired but no ActivityBatchCompleted followed within the reconciliation window. Emitted by ReconcileActivityBatchesJob on the orphan sweep — informational, not raised to a caller.

BackfillActivityTenantIdFromJsonbJob --verify found rows where tenant_id IS NULL AND properties->>'tenant_id' IS NULL. Alerts on-call — an activity row that pre-dates the backfill has no way to determine its owner tenant. Must be resolved manually before the Wave-6 upgrade is complete.
